originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
content: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

originalTitle: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: A Special Tree Helps Hungry Tummies in Africa!
content:
Hello, amazing explorers! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, ready to share some important news from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something a little bit serious, but also about a special tree that is helping many people. Let's learn together!

What's Happening in Africa?

Imagine waking up every day and the sun is shining super bright, but no rain falls from the sky. Not for a day, not for a week, but for many, many months! This is called a "drought." When there's a drought, the ground gets very dry and hard. Plants can't grow, and animals don't have water to drink. It's a very sad time. Right now, in some parts of Africa, like Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia, there has been a very long drought. It's been dry for a really, really long time. This means it's hard for farmers to grow yummy food like maize, beans, and vegetables. Without rain, rivers can shrink, and water holes dry up. This makes it very difficult for people and animals to find fresh water. Animals can get very thirsty and sick. It's a big problem for everyone. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent, much bigger than many other places! It has many different types of weather, but some parts are facing a big challenge right now because of this long dry spell. The land can become very dusty, almost like a desert.

The Hungry Tummy Problem

When there's no rain and no food grows, people can get very, very hungry. Sometimes, they don't have enough food for their tummies, and this is called "extreme hunger." It means they might not have breakfast, lunch, or dinner. Their tummies rumble and feel empty. Right now, a group that helps people, called Oxfam, says that a super big number of people – like 26 million people! – are feeling this extreme hunger in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. That's like many, many school playgrounds full of kids and grown-ups who need food. Imagine trying to play or learn when your tummy feels so empty. It's very hard. Kids need good food to grow strong, to learn new things, and to run and play. When food is scarce, it's difficult to be energetic and healthy. This is why finding food is super important for families living in these areas. They are looking for anything they can find to eat.

Meet the Gingerbread Tree!

But guess what? Sometimes, nature has special surprises! People in Kenya have found a special tree that is helping them. It's called the "Gingerbread Tree." Isn't that a fun name? It sounds like a yummy cookie! This tree usually grows in places that are quite dry. It's a very strong tree that can live even when there isn't much water. Its scientific name is *Hyphaene compressa*, but most people just call it the gingerbread tree because its fruit smells and tastes a little bit like gingerbread cookies! The gingerbread tree has big, fan-shaped leaves, and it grows tall, sometimes up to 15 meters high! It gives us fruit that looks a bit like small coconuts or brown-orange apples. These fruits grow in big bunches on the tree. Did you know? The gingerbread tree is sometimes also called the "doum palm" or "mkoma" in Swahili! It's very common in parts of East Africa and has been used by people for many things for a long, long time, not just for food but also for building and making crafts.

Why is This Tree So Special Now?

Normally, people might eat the fruit of the gingerbread tree sometimes, as a little treat or snack, but it's not their main food. But because of the super long drought and not enough regular food, people are now relying on this special tree a lot more. They carefully pick the ripe fruits from the tree. These fruits have a hard outer shell, but inside there's a yummy, fibrous pulp that people can chew. It might not be a full meal, but it helps to fill those hungry tummies and gives them some energy. It's like when you have a snack between meals – it helps you keep going until the next big meal. For many people right now, the gingerbread tree fruit is their main snack, or even their main food, for a day. It helps them feel less hungry. It shows how clever people are, finding ways to survive and find food even in very difficult times. They are resourceful and know how to use what nature gives them. This tree is a true gift during hard times.

Who is Helping?

There are many kind people and groups who want to help. One of them is called Oxfam. Oxfam is like a big team of helpers who work all over the world. They try to make sure everyone has enough food, clean water, and a safe place to live. Oxfam is telling everyone that many people need help right now in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. They are trying to get food, water, and other important things to the people who need them most. They work hard to bring trucks full of supplies to remote villages. They also help people learn how to grow food in smarter ways, even when there isn't much rain, or how to get water from deeper underground using special tools. This helps people be ready for future dry times. Did you know? There are many amazing groups like Oxfam that work hard every day to make the world a better place for everyone! They have many brave helpers who travel to help people in need.

What Can We Do?

It can be a little sad to hear about people who are hungry. But we can all help in small ways! We can talk about it with our families and friends so more people know about the challenge. When more people know, more people can help. We can be extra thankful for the food we have on our plates every day and try not to waste any. Every little piece of food is precious. We can also learn about different ways to save water, because water is super precious for everyone, everywhere. Turning off the tap while brushing your teeth is one small way to help. And most importantly, we can share kindness and think about others. When we are kind, we make the world a brighter place for everyone. A little kindness can go a long way.

A Message of Hope

Even though the drought is a big problem, there is always hope. People are strong, and they are working together to find solutions and help each other. And special trees like the gingerbread tree are showing us how nature can help too. Many people around the world care and are trying their best to send help to our friends in Africa. We are all part of one big global family. Let's keep hoping for rain in these dry lands and for happy, full tummies for all our friends in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia! Thank you for listening, my amazing explorers! See you next time!
summary: People in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia are very hungry because there has been no rain for a long time, but a special 'gingerbread tree' is helping them find food.
whyMatters: This is important because many children and families need food and water to be healthy and strong, and we can learn how people are helping each other during tough times.
